issuesFound.title: Removing default focus indicators
issuesFound.explanation: The CSS removes the default browser focus outline for all elements using `:focus-visible { outline: none; }` and `:focus:not(:focus-visible) { outline: none; }`. While a custom outline is defined, it uses a fixed color (`#aaaaaa` from the variable) which may not provide sufficient contrast against all background colors in the document, potentially leaving keyboard users without a visible insertion point.
issuesFound.fix: Line 298: `:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid currentColor; outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }`

issuesFound.title: Insufficient body text size
issuesFound.explanation: The root font size is set to 12px, which is significantly below the recommended 16px (1rem) for body text. This forces users with visual impairments to zoom the page immediately to achieve a readable text size.
issuesFound.fix: Line 168: `font-size: 16px;`

issuesFound.title: Insufficient line height on paragraphs
issuesFound.explanation: Paragraph elements have a line-height of 1, which is below the WCAG minimum of 1.5 for body text. This causes text lines to crowd together, severely impacting readability for users with dyslexia or low vision.
issuesFound.fix: Line 204: `line-height: 1.5;`

issuesFound.title: Missing prefers-reduced-motion guard for smooth scrolling
issuesFound.explanation: The `scroll-behavior: smooth` property is applied globally to the html element. While a media query exists on line 176, it only sets it to `auto` for the `html` tag, but other transitions (like the `.skip-link` on line 378 or `.btn` on line 514) do not have corresponding overrides in this file to disable motion for users with vestibular disorders.
issuesFound.fix: Line 845: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { *, ::before, ::after { animation-duration: 0.01ms !important; animation-iteration-count: 1 !important; transition-duration: 0.01ms !important; scroll-behavior: auto !important; } }`

issuesFound.title: Insufficient color contrast for secondary text
issuesFound.explanation: The variable `--colour-text-secondary` is set to `#aaaaaa` and used against a white background (`--colour-neutral-0` / `#ffffff`). This produces a contrast ratio of 2.3:1, which fails the WCAG AA requirement of 4.5:1 for normal text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 68: `--colour-text-secondary: #6b7280;`

issuesFound.title: Insufficient color contrast for error text
issuesFound.explanation: The variable `--colour-error` is set to `#ee9999`. When used for the `.required-marker` on line 405 against a white background, the contrast ratio is approximately 2.1:1, failing the 4.5:1 requirement for informative text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 54: `--colour-error: #dc2626;`

issuesFound.title: Hidden search label
issuesFound.explanation: The CSS explicitly sets `display: none` on the search label. Even if the label has the `sr-only` class, `display: none` removes the element from the accessibility tree entirely, leaving the search input without an accessible name for screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 665: `#site-search label.sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0; }`

issuesFound.title: Ineffective screen-reader-only utility
issuesFound.explanation: The `.sr-only` class uses `position: static`, `width: auto`, and `height: auto`. This fails to visually hide the content, meaning text intended only for screen readers will clutter the visual interface, and it does not follow the standard "clip" pattern required to hide content while keeping it accessible.
issuesFound.fix: Line 334: `.sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0; }`

issuesFound.title: Insufficient focus indicator contrast on search input
issuesFound.explanation: The `#search-input:focus` state uses a `box-shadow` with an alpha transparency (`rgba(0, 95, 204, 0.2)`). This light blue glow does not meet the 3:1 non-text contrast requirement against the white background, making it difficult for keyboard users to identify the focused input.
issuesFound.fix: Line 688: `#search-input:focus { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 2px; }`

issuesFound.title: Fixed height on search input
issuesFound.explanation: The search input has a fixed height of 28px. If a user increases the text size or line height via browser settings, the text will likely be clipped vertically, violating WCAG 1.4.4 (Resize Text).
issuesFound.fix: Line 669: `#search-input { min-height: 44px; padding: var(--space-2) var(--space-3); }`

issuesFound.title: Removing focus indicators without alternative
Missing keyboard handler for click event on non-native button
issuesFound.explanation: The `dismissBtn` is created as a `<button>` but the `on` utility is used to attach only a `click` event. While native buttons trigger click on Enter/Space, the custom `on` wrapper and the manual `activeToasts` management in this script may not consistently handle keyboard activation if the browser's default behavior is intercepted or if the element is replaced.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1022: `on(dismissBtn, 'click keydown', (e) => { if (e.type === 'click' || e.key === 'Enter' || e.key === ' ') dismiss(toast); });`

issuesFound.title: Missing ARIA live region for dynamic content injection
issuesFound.explanation: The `replaceWith` function allows replacing a DOM element with an HTML string. If this is used to update visible page state (e.g., a loading spinner or a status message), there is no logic to ensure the change is announced to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1194: `if (typeof replacement === 'string') { target.insertAdjacentHTML('afterend', replacement); const next = target.nextElementSibling; if (next) { next.setAttribute('aria-live', 'polite'); } target.parentNode.removeChild(target); }`

issuesFound.title: Missing focus management on modal/drawer open
issuesFound.explanation: The `open` function for the mobile navigation drawer adds classes and locks scroll but does not programmatically move focus into the drawer. Keyboard users will remain on the trigger button while the menu opens behind/over the content.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1645: `function open(announceToSR) { ... _lockScroll(); const firstLink = getFocusableChildren(_drawer)[0]; if (firstLink) firstLink.focus(); emit(_trigger, 'mobilenav:open', { drawer: _drawer }); }`

issuesFound.title: Missing focus return on modal/drawer close
issuesFound.explanation: The `close` function for the mobile navigation drawer unlocks scroll and removes classes but does not return focus to the `_trigger` element. Focus may be lost or reset to the top of the document when the drawer is hidden.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1661: `function close(announceToSR) { ... _unlockScroll(); if (_trigger) _trigger.focus(); emit(_trigger, 'mobilenav:close', { drawer: _drawer }); }`

issuesFound.title: Missing keyboard handler for custom toggle switch
issuesFound.explanation: The `_toggleEl` in the pricing module is given `role="switch"` and `tabindex="0"` if it is not a button, but it only has a `click` listener. Non-button elements do not trigger `click` on Space or Enter by default.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2345: `_toggleEl.addEventListener('keydown', (e) => { if (e.key === ' ' || e.key === 'Enter') { e.preventDefault(); _onToggleClick(); } });`

issuesFound.title: Missing ARIA live region for search suggestion updates
issuesFound.explanation: The `render` function in `suggestionListRenderer` updates the `innerHTML` of the search suggestions list. While a status element exists, the list itself should be identified as a live region or the status should explicitly announce when the list is populated.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2085: `_listEl.innerHTML = items.map((item, i) => _itemHtml(item, i, query)).join(''); _listEl.setAttribute('aria-live', 'polite'); show();`

issuesFound.title: Missing pause/stop mechanism for auto-playing carousel
issuesFound.explanation: The `play` function initiates a `setTimeout` loop (`_scheduleNext`) that automatically changes visible content. While there is a `pause` function, WCAG 2.2.1 requires a user-controllable mechanism to pause, stop, or hide content that rotates automatically.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2685: `function play() { if (prefersReducedMotion()) return; _playing = true; _scheduleNext(); /* Ensure a visible stop button is rendered and linked to pause() */ }`

issuesFound.title: Use of innerHTML with untrusted content
issuesFound.explanation: The `_itemHtml` function uses `item.icon` and `labelHtml` (which contains `<mark>` tags) inside `innerHTML`. If the suggestion data source contains unsanitized strings, this creates an XSS risk which can be used to bypass accessibility controls.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2058: `const labelSpan = createElement('span', { className: 'suggestion__label' }); labelSpan.textContent = item.label; // Use textContent instead of innerHTML for raw labels`

issuesFound.title: Missing accessible name for scroll-to-top button
issuesFound.explanation: The `_btn` for scroll-to-top is retrieved via selector but the script does not verify or set an accessible name (aria-label or text content) if it is missing in the markup.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1855: `if (!_btn.getAttribute('aria-label') && !_btn.textContent) { _btn.setAttribute('aria-label', STRINGS.labels.scrollToTop || 'Scroll to top'); }`

issuesFound.title: Missing keyboard handler for click delegation on FAQ triggers
issuesFound.explanation: The `_onContainerClick` handler in `faqAccordion` uses `e.target.closest` to detect clicks on triggers. If the trigger is a `<div>` or `<span>` (as allowed by the logic on line 2445), it will not respond to Enter or Space keys.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2515: `_containerEl.addEventListener('keydown', (e) => { if (e.key === ' ' || e.key === 'Enter') { const trigger = e.target.closest('[data-faq-trigger]'); if (trigger) { e.preventDefault(); const item = faqRegistry.getAll().find(i => i.trigger === trigger); if (item) toggle(item); } } });`

fpTitles: Missing keyboard handler for click event on non-native button
Missing pause/stop mechanism for auto-playing carousel
Use of innerHTML with untrusted content

issuesFound.title: Missing keyboard handler for click event on non-native button
issuesFound.explanation: The `dismissBtn` is created as a `<button>` but the `on` utility is used to attach only a `click` event. While native buttons trigger click on Enter/Space, custom event listeners on elements injected via JS often require explicit keydown handling if the browser's default behavior is bypassed or if the element is treated as a custom widget.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1022: `on(dismissBtn, 'click keydown', (e) => { if (e.type === 'click' || e.key === 'Enter' || e.key === ' ') dismiss(toast); });`

issuesFound.title: Missing ARIA live region for dynamic content injection
issuesFound.explanation: The `replaceWith` function allows replacing a DOM element with an HTML string or new element. If this change updates visible page state (like a status message or search result), it will not be announced to screen readers because there is no associated ARIA live region or status role.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1195: `if (typeof replacement === 'string') { target.insertAdjacentHTML('afterend', replacement); liveRegion.announce('Content updated'); target.parentNode.removeChild(target); }`

issuesFound.title: Missing keyboard handler for click event on custom tab implementation
issuesFound.explanation: The `_onClick` handler for the roving tabindex pattern is attached only to the `click` event. Users navigating via keyboard will be able to move focus using arrow keys (handled in `_onKeydown`), but pressing Enter or Space on an item will not trigger the `roving:select` logic because the click listener does not capture keyboard activation on non-button roles.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1615: `container.addEventListener('click', _onClick); container.addEventListener('keydown', (e) => { if (e.key === 'Enter' || e.key === ' ') _onClick(e); });`

issuesFound.title: Focus not returned to trigger on mobile menu close
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation menu is closed via the `close()` function, focus is not explicitly returned to the `_trigger` element. This causes keyboard focus to be lost or reset to the top of the document, forcing the user to navigate the entire page again.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1814: `if (_trap && _trap.isActive()) _trap.deactivate(); if (_trigger) _trigger.focus();`

issuesFound.title: Missing keyboard handler for click event on dropdown hover wrapper
issuesFound.explanation: The `mouseenter` and `mouseleave` listeners handle opening/closing dropdowns for mouse users, but there is no equivalent logic for keyboard users to trigger the dropdown via the wrapper if the trigger itself is bypassed or if the wrapper is intended to be interactive.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2018: `wrapper.addEventListener('mouseenter', () => _onHover('enter', inst)); wrapper.addEventListener('mouseleave', () => _onHover('leave', inst)); wrapper.addEventListener('keydown', (e) => { if (e.key === 'Enter') _onHover('enter', inst); });`

issuesFound.title: Missing ARIA live region for search results update
issuesFound.explanation: The `_fetchAndRender` function updates the `suggestionListRenderer` with new results. While a status element is created later in `init`, the immediate rendering of results does not trigger a live region announcement, meaning screen reader users are not informed that the list of suggestions has changed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2245: `suggestionListRenderer.render(results, norm); liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.searchResultsFound(results.length, norm));`

issuesFound.title: Missing keyboard handler for click event on filter chips
issuesFound.explanation: The `_onChipClick` handler is attached only to the `click` event on the container. While the chips contain buttons, the delegated listener on the container may not consistently capture keyboard "click" events (Enter/Space) depending on how the event bubbles from the custom inner HTML.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2615: `_containerEl.addEventListener('click', _onChipClick); _containerEl.addEventListener('keydown', (e) => { if (e.key === 'Enter' || e.key === ' ') _onChipClick(e); });`

issuesFound.title: Missing keyboard handler for click event on pricing toggle
issuesFound.explanation: The `_toggleEl` is given a `keydown` listener for Space/Enter, but the `_onToggleClick` is only bound to `click`. If the element is a `<div>` (as suggested by the `tabindex` check), some browsers will not synthesize a click event on Enter/Space, leaving the component inaccessible to keyboard users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2895: `_toggleEl.addEventListener('click', _onToggleClick); _toggleEl.addEventListener('keydown', (e) => { if (e.key === 'Enter' || e.key === ' ') { e.preventDefault(); _onToggleClick(); } });`

issuesFound.title: Missing keyboard handler for click event on comparison table headers
issuesFound.explanation: The delegated `click` listener on `[data-plan-head] a, [data-plan-head] button` handles the scroll-to-plan logic. If the user activates these elements via keyboard, the `click` event might not fire correctly if the event is intercepted or if the target is a non-button element acting as a trigger.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3035: `delegate(_tableEl, '[data-plan-head] a, [data-plan-head] button', 'click', e => { ... }); delegate(_tableEl, '[data-plan-head] a, [data-plan-head] button', 'keydown', e => { if (e.key === 'Enter' || e.key === ' ') { e.preventDefault(); scrollToPlan(planId); } });`

issuesFound.title: Missing keyboard handler for click event on FAQ category headers
issuesFound.explanation: The `_onContainerClick` delegated listener only looks for triggers. If category headers are intended to be interactive (e.g., to collapse a whole section), they lack keyboard support.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3265: `_containerEl.addEventListener('click', _onContainerClick); _containerEl.addEventListener('keydown', (e) => { if (e.key === 'Enter' || e.key === ' ') _onContainerClick(e); });`

issuesFound.title: Missing keyboard handler for click event on carousel dots
issuesFound.explanation: The `_onDotsClick` listener is attached only to the `click` event. Since the dots are buttons with `role="tab"`, they must also respond to keyboard activation (Enter/Space) to be WCAG compliant.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3535: `_dotsEl.addEventListener('click', _onDotsClick); _dotsEl.addEventListener('keydown', (e) => { if (e.key === 'Enter' || e.key === ' ') _onDotsClick(e); });`

issuesFound.title: Missing keyboard handler for click event on file upload zone
issuesFound.explanation: The `zoneEl` has a `keydown` listener for Enter/Space, but it only triggers `inputEl.click()`. If the `click` event on the `zoneEl` itself is used for other logic (like focus management), it is not consistently handled for keyboard users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4185: `zoneEl.addEventListener('keydown', (e) => { if (e.key === 'Enter' || e.key === ' ') { e.preventDefault(); inputEl.click(); } });`

issuesFound.title: Missing keyboard handler for click event on theme toggle buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The `_onThemeClick` delegated listener is attached only to the `click` event. If the `[data-theme-toggle]` elements are not native `<button>` elements, they will not be activatable via keyboard.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4855: `document.addEventListener('click', _onThemeClick); document.addEventListener('keydown', (e) => { if (e.key === 'Enter' || e.key === ' ') _onThemeClick(e); });`

issuesFound.title: Auto-changing content without pause/stop control
issuesFound.explanation: The `play()` function in `testimonialsCarousel` starts a `setTimeout` loop that automatically changes slides. While a `pause()` function exists, WCAG 2.2.1 requires that users have a clear, always-available mechanism to stop or pause any auto-updating content that lasts longer than 5 seconds.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3465: `function play() { if (prefersReducedMotion()) return; _playing = true; _scheduleNext(); /* Ensure a visible pause button is always rendered and accessible */ }`

issuesFound.title: Missing ARIA live region for form success message
issuesFound.explanation: In `newsletterForm`, the `_showSuccess` function updates the `_statusEl` text and removes the `hidden` attribute. While it calls `liveRegion.announce`, the `_statusEl` itself should have `role="status"` or `aria-live="polite"` to ensure the message is persistent and discoverable.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3885: `_statusEl = createElement('div', { class: 'newsletter-status', 'role': 'status', 'aria-live': 'polite' });`

issuesFound.title: Missing keyboard handler for click event on non-native button
issuesFound.explanation: The `dismissBtn` is created as a `<button>` but the `on` utility is used to attach only a `click` event. While native buttons trigger click on Enter/Space, custom event listeners on elements injected via JS often require explicit keydown handling if the browser's default behavior is bypassed or if the element is treated as a custom widget.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1022: `on(dismissBtn, 'click keydown', (e) => { if (e.type === 'click' || e.key === 'Enter' || e.key === ' ') dismiss(toast); });`

issuesFound.title: Missing ARIA live region for dynamic content injection
issuesFound.explanation: The `replaceWith` function allows replacing a DOM element with an HTML string or new element. If this change updates visible page state (like a status message or search result), it will not be announced to screen readers because there is no associated ARIA live region or status role.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1195: `if (typeof replacement === 'string') { target.insertAdjacentHTML('afterend', replacement); liveRegion.announce('Content updated'); target.parentNode.removeChild(target); }`

issuesFound.title: Missing keyboard handler for click event on custom tab implementation
issuesFound.explanation: The `_onClick` handler for the roving tabindex pattern is attached only to the `click` event. Users navigating via keyboard will be able to move focus using arrow keys (handled in `_onKeydown`), but pressing Enter or Space on an item will not trigger the `roving:select` logic because the click listener does not capture keyboard activation on non-button roles.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1615: `container.addEventListener('click', _onClick); container.addEventListener('keydown', (e) => { if (e.key === 'Enter' || e.key === ' ') _onClick(e); });`

issuesFound.title: Focus not returned to trigger on mobile menu close
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation menu is closed via the `close()` function, focus is not explicitly returned to the `_trigger` element. This causes keyboard focus to be lost or reset to the top of the document, forcing the user to navigate the entire page again.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1814: `if (_trap && _trap.isActive()) _trap.deactivate(); if (_trigger) _trigger.focus();`

issuesFound.title: Missing keyboard handler for click event on dropdown hover wrapper
issuesFound.explanation: The `mouseenter` and `mouseleave` listeners handle opening/closing dropdowns for mouse users, but there is no equivalent logic for keyboard users to trigger the dropdown via the wrapper if the trigger itself is bypassed or if the wrapper is intended to be interactive.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2018: `wrapper.addEventListener('mouseenter', () => _onHover('enter', inst)); wrapper.addEventListener('mouseleave', () => _onHover('leave', inst)); wrapper.addEventListener('keydown', (e) => { if (e.key === 'Enter') _onHover('enter', inst); });`

issuesFound.title: Missing ARIA live region for search results update
issuesFound.explanation: The `_fetchAndRender` function updates the `suggestionListRenderer` with new results. While a status element is created later in `init`, the immediate rendering of results does not trigger a live region announcement, meaning screen reader users are not informed that the list of suggestions has changed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2245: `suggestionListRenderer.render(results, norm); liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.searchResultsFound(results.length, norm));`

issuesFound.title: Missing keyboard handler for click event on filter chips
issuesFound.explanation: The `_onChipClick` handler is attached only to the `click` event on the container. While the chips contain buttons, the delegated listener on the container may not consistently capture keyboard "click" events (Enter/Space) depending on how the event bubbles from the custom inner HTML.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2615: `_containerEl.addEventListener('click', _onChipClick); _containerEl.addEventListener('keydown', (e) => { if (e.key === 'Enter' || e.key === ' ') _onChipClick(e); });`

issuesFound.title: Missing keyboard handler for click event on pricing toggle
issuesFound.explanation: The `_toggleEl` is given a `keydown` listener for Space/Enter, but the `_onToggleClick` is only bound to `click`. If the element is a `<div>` (as suggested by the `tabindex` check), some browsers will not synthesize a click event on Enter/Space, leaving the component inaccessible to keyboard users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2895: `_toggleEl.addEventListener('click', _onToggleClick); _toggleEl.addEventListener('keydown', (e) => { if (e.key === 'Enter' || e.key === ' ') { e.preventDefault(); _onToggleClick(); } });`

issuesFound.title: Missing keyboard handler for click event on comparison table headers
issuesFound.explanation: The delegated `click` listener on `[data-plan-head] a, [data-plan-head] button` handles the scroll-to-plan logic. If the user activates these elements via keyboard, the `click` event might not fire correctly if the event is intercepted or if the target is a non-button element acting as a trigger.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3035: `delegate(_tableEl, '[data-plan-head] a, [data-plan-head] button', 'click', e => { ... }); delegate(_tableEl, '[data-plan-head] a, [data-plan-head] button', 'keydown', e => { if (e.key === 'Enter' || e.key === ' ') { e.preventDefault(); scrollToPlan(planId); } });`

issuesFound.title: Missing page title
issuesFound.explanation: The document is missing a <title> element. Users of assistive technology rely on the page title to identify the site and page purpose quickly without navigating the body content.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1: <head><title>Luminary Studio — Accessible Design and Productivity Tools</title></head>

issuesFound.title: Missing href on anchor
issuesFound.explanation: The anchor element used for the mobile navigation close button is missing an href attribute. Without an href, the element is not a valid link and is not reachable in the keyboard tab order.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1014: <IconButton as="button" onClick={onClose} className="mobile-nav__header-close">

issuesFound.title: Missing accessible name on icon button
issuesFound.explanation: The IconButton used to close the mobile navigation contains only a "✕" character and has no aria-label or visually hidden text. Screen reader users will hear "times" or "multiplication sign" instead of the button's function.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1014: <IconButton aria-label="Close navigation menu" onClick={onClose} className="mobile-nav__close">

issuesFound.title: Missing accessible name on anchor
issuesFound.explanation: The GitHub social media link in the footer contains an empty span and has no text content or aria-label.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1346: <a href={s.href} className="site-footer__social-link" aria-label={s.label} rel="noreferrer noopener" target="_blank">

issuesFound.title: Broken ARIA reference
issuesFound.explanation: The NotificationContainer references a "dismiss" function from context that is not defined in the provided NotificationContextValue interface or provider. This will cause a runtime error when attempting to dismiss a toast.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1488: const { notifications, removeNotification } = useContext(NotificationContext);

issuesFound.title: Missing form label association
issuesFound.explanation: The NewsletterForm uses a custom FormGroup but the TextInput inside is not programmatically linked to the label because the "error" prop logic in FormGroup does not match the "errorId" expected by the TextInput's aria-describedby.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1188: <FormGroup label="Email address" htmlFor="newsletter-email" errorId="newsletter-email-error" errorMessage={email.state.touched ? email.state.error : undefined}>

issuesFound.title: Missing autocomplete on personal data input
issuesFound.explanation: The "Full name" input in the ContactForm is missing the autocomplete attribute. WCAG 2.2 requires autocomplete for inputs that collect personal data to assist users with cognitive disabilities.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1248: <TextInput id="contact-name" name="name" type="text" autoComplete="name" ... />

issuesFound.title: Focusable element inside aria-hidden region
issuesFound.explanation: The background video in the HeroSection has aria-hidden="true" but retains tabIndex={-1}. While -1 removes it from the natural tab order, any programmatic focus or accidental inclusion of focusable children inside this div would be hidden from screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1145: <video ref={videoRef} aria-hidden="true" muted playsInline className="hero-section__video" />

issuesFound.title: Non-standard keyboard interaction
issuesFound.explanation: The TestimonialsCarousel uses arrow keys for navigation but does not implement a roving tabindex. This means a keyboard user must tab through every single dot/control even if they are not interacting with the carousel.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1065: (Implement useRovingTabindex on the dot navigation buttons)

'contact-name-error' : undefined} />

issuesFound.title: Broken aria-controls reference
issuesFound.explanation: The NotificationToast component uses a dismiss function that is not defined in the local context; it references "dismiss" instead of "removeNotification" from the NotificationContext. This prevents screen reader users from successfully triggering the dismissal of the notification.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1685: const { notifications, removeNotification } = useContext(NotificationContext); ... <NotificationToast key={n.id} notification={n} onDismiss={removeNotification} />

issuesFound.title: Missing accessible name on icon button
issuesFound.explanation: The close button in the MobileNav component contains only the character "✕" and lacks an aria-label or visually hidden text. Screen reader users will hear "times" or "unlabelled button" instead of the button's purpose.
issuesFound.fix: Line 915: <IconButton onClick={onClose} className="mobile-nav__close" aria-label="Close navigation menu"> ✕ </IconButton>

issuesFound.title: Focusable element inside aria-hidden container
issuesFound.explanation: The NavDropdown component places the submenu <ul> inside an <li> that remains in the DOM. When the menu is closed, the <ul> is hidden with the `hidden` attribute, but if CSS fails to apply `display: none`, the `aria-hidden` state of the parent or the `hidden` attribute might not prevent focus if children are not handled. More specifically, the "SiteSearch" form on line 1045 uses `autoComplete="off"` on a search input which is acceptable, but the "NewsletterForm" on line 1365 contains a honeypot input with `tabIndex={-1}` inside an `aria-hidden="true"` div, which is correct, however, the "IntegrationsBar" on line 1185 contains a clone list for the marquee that is not hidden from the tab order.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1185:
    {INTEGRATIONS.map((item) => (
  • {item.name}
    • ))}

issuesFound.title: Missing group label for star rating
issuesFound.explanation: The StarRating component uses a span with an aria-label to describe the rating, but the individual star characters (★/☆) are not hidden from screen readers. This results in the screen reader announcing the label followed by "star star star...".
issuesFound.fix: Line 615: {filled ? '★' : '☆'}

issuesFound.title: Missing list semantics for social links
issuesFound.explanation: The social media links in the footer are wrapped in a `<nav>` but the icons themselves are rendered as empty `<span>` elements with `aria-hidden="true"`. While the `<a>` has an `aria-label`, the visual icons provide no feedback if CSS fails to load.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1585: <span aria-hidden="true" className="social-icon" role="presentation" />

issuesFound.title: Inconsistent heading hierarchy
issuesFound.explanation: The FooterNavGroup uses an `<h2>` for its heading. Since the footer follows sections that use `<h2>` (like FAQ and Newsletter), these footer headings should ideally be at a lower level or the footer should be wrapped in a way that defines its relationship to the page structure to avoid skipping levels if the main content ends with an `<h3>`.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1555:

{group.heading}

issuesFound.title: Missing form association
issuesFound.explanation: The NewsletterForm uses a `FormGroup` which expects an `errorId` to link the error message to the input via `aria-describedby`. The `TextInput` on line 1405 manually checks for `email.state.error` but the `FormGroup` itself does not receive the `errorId` prop, meaning the `ErrorMessage` component's ID won't match the `aria-describedby` on the input.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1385: <FormGroup label="Email address" htmlFor="newsletter-email" errorId="newsletter-email-error" errorMessage={email.state.touched ? email.state.error : undefined} >

issuesFound.title: Missing accessible name for search input
issuesFound.explanation: The search input element referenced by SELECTORS.searchInput ('#search-input') is missing an accessible name. Without a label, screen reader users cannot understand the purpose of the input. This violates WCAG 2.5.3 Label in Name.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1: Add an aria-label attribute to the search input element in the HTML, e.g., <input id="search-input" aria-label="Search the site">. Alternatively, add a visible <label> element associated with the input.

issuesFound.title: Missing accessible name for newsletter email input

issuesFound.title: Missing accessible name for contact form inputs
issuesFound.explanation: Multiple contact form input elements (e.g., '#contact-first-name', '#contact-last-name', '#contact-email', '#contact-company', '#contact-team-size', '#contact-message') are missing accessible names. Without labels, screen reader users cannot understand the purpose of each field, violating WCAG 2.5.3.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1: For each contact form input, add an aria-label attribute or a visible <label> element in the HTML. For example, <input id="contact-first-name" aria-label="First name">.

issuesFound.title: Missing accessible name for cookie settings button
issuesFound.explanation: The cookie settings button referenced by SELECTORS.cookieSettingsBtn ('#cookie-settings-btn') is missing an accessible name. Screen reader users will not know the button's purpose, violating WCAG 2.5.3.

issuesFound.title: Missing accessible name for scroll-to-top button
issuesFound.explanation: The scroll-to-top button referenced by SELECTORS.scrollTopBtn ('#scroll-top-btn') is missing an accessible name. Screen reader users will not know the button's purpose, violating WCAG 2.5.3.

issuesFound.title: Missing accessible name for pricing toggle buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing toggle buttons referenced by SELECTORS.billingMonthly ('#billing-monthly') and SELECTORS.billingAnnual ('#billing-annual') are missing accessible names. Screen reader users will not know the purpose of these toggle buttons, violating WCAG 2.5.3.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1: Add aria-label attributes to the pricing toggle buttons in the HTML, e.g., <button id="billing-monthly" aria-label="Switch to monthly billing"> and <button id="billing-annual" aria-label="Switch to annual billing">.

issuesFound.title: Missing accessible name for FAQ toggle buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ toggle buttons referenced by SELECTORS.faqTriggers ('.faq-item dt button') are missing accessible names. Screen reader users will not know which question is being expanded/collapsed, violating WCAG 2.5.3.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1: For each FAQ toggle button, add an aria-label attribute in the HTML that includes the question text, e.g., <button aria-label="Expand answer for [question text]">.

issuesFound.title: Missing accessible name for product filter tabs
issuesFound.explanation: The product filter tabs referenced by SELECTORS.filterTabs ('.filter-tabs button') are missing accessible names. Screen reader users will not know the purpose of each filter tab, violating WCAG 2.5.3.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1: Add aria-label attributes to each filter tab button in the HTML, e.g., <button aria-label="Filter by [category name]">.

issuesFound.title: Missing accessible name for testimonial carousel buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The testimonial carousel previous and next buttons referenced by SELECTORS.carouselPrev and SELECTORS.carouselNext (not defined in SELECTORS but implied) are missing accessible names. Screen reader users will not know the purpose of these buttons, violating WCAG 2.5.3.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1: Add aria-label attributes to the carousel buttons in the HTML, e.g., <button aria-label="Previous testimonial"> and <button aria-label="Next testimonial">.

issuesFound.title: Missing accessible name for toast dismiss buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The toast dismiss buttons created in the toastManager module are missing accessible names. The code sets an aria-label (STRINGS.labels.dismissToast), but if STRINGS.labels.dismissToast is undefined, the button will have no accessible name, violating WCAG 2.5.3.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1: Ensure STRINGS.labels.dismissToast is defined in the STRINGS object. For example, add dismissToast: 'Dismiss notification' to STRINGS.labels.

issuesFound.title: Missing keyboard event handler for click event on non-button element
issuesFound.explanation: The code uses addEventListener for 'click' events on various elements (e.g., in mobileNav, dropdownMenu, etc.), but for non-button elements that are made interactive via click, there is no corresponding keyboard event handler (e.g., for Enter or Space keys). This violates WCAG 2.1.1 Keyboard.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1: For each non-button element that has a click event listener, add a keydown event handler that responds to Enter and Space keys. For example, in the mobileNav module, add a keydown handler to the trigger element that calls the same function as the click handler when Enter or Space is pressed.

issuesFound.title: Missing autocomplete attribute for personal data inputs
issuesFound.explanation: The contact form inputs for personal data (e.g., name, email, company) are missing autocomplete attributes. This makes it harder for users, especially those with cognitive disabilities, to fill out the form, violating WCAG 1.3.5 Identify Input Purpose.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1: Add appropriate autocomplete attributes to the contact form inputs in the HTML. For example, <input id="contact-first-name" autocomplete="given-name">, <input id="contact-last-name" autocomplete="family-name">, <input id="contact-email" autocomplete="email">, <input id="contact-company" autocomplete="organization">.

issuesFound.title: Missing role for progress bar
issuesFound.explanation: The progress bar element referenced by SELECTORS.progressBar (not defined in SELECTORS but implied) is missing the role="progressbar" attribute and associated aria-valuenow, aria-valuemin, aria-valuemax attributes. Screen reader users will not be able to perceive the progress bar, violating WCAG 1.3.1 Info and Relationships.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1: Add role="progressbar", aria-valuenow, aria-valuemin, and aria-valuemax attributes to the progress bar element in the HTML. For example, <div role="progressbar" aria-valuenow="50" aria-valuemin="0" aria-valuemax="100">.

issuesFound.title: Missing live region for dynamic content updates
issuesFound.explanation: The code uses innerHTML or insertAdjacentHTML to update content dynamically (e.g., in search suggestions, product filtering), but there is no adjacent ARIA live region to announce these changes to screen reader users. This violates WCAG 4.1.3 Status Messages.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1: Ensure that dynamic content updates are announced via an ARIA live region. For example, in the siteSearch module, the statusEl element is used as a live region, but it must be present in the HTML and have appropriate aria-live attributes.

issuesFound.title: Missing focus management for modal dialogs
issuesFound.explanation: The modal dialog elements (e.g., cookie consent banner, mobile navigation drawer) do not have programmatic focus management when opened. Focus should be moved to the modal when it opens and returned to the trigger when it closes, to comply with WCAG 2.4.3 Focus Order.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1: In the cookieConsent and mobileNav modules, ensure that focus is moved to the modal element when it opens and returned to the trigger element when it closes. Use the focus() method and manage focus traps.

Note: The line numbers are set to 1 because the actual HTML elements are not present in the JavaScript code. The fixes must be applied in the HTML markup that uses this JavaScript. The issues are identified based on the selectors and functionality defined in the JavaScript.

issuesFound.title: :focus-visible removes native outline without providing a visible focus indicator
issuesFound.explanation: The rule `:focus-visible { outline:none; … }` eliminates the default focus ring for all interactive elements, leaving keyboard users without any visual cue of focus. This blocks access to focusable controls (WCAG 2.4.3).
issuesFound.fix:
Add a visible style (e.g., a box‑shadow) to the selector.  
Line 435: `:focus-visible { outline: none; outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px var(--colour-focus-ring); }`

issuesFound.title: .btn:focus-visible removes outline without visible replacement
issuesFound.explanation: Buttons lose their focus indicator because `outline:none` is applied and no alternative visual style is defined, violating focus‑visibility requirements.
issuesFound.fix:
Add a visible focus style.  
Line 728: `.btn:focus-visible { outline: none; outline-offset: 3px; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px var(--colour-focus-ring); }`

issuesFound.title: .faq-item dt button:focus-visible removes outline without visible replacement
issuesFound.explanation: Accordion buttons lose their focus indicator (`outline:none`) with no alternative visual cue, breaking keyboard navigation.
issuesFound.fix:
Add a visible focus style.  
Line 1101: `.faq-item dt button:focus-visible { outline: none; outline-offset: -3px; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px var(--colour-focus-ring); }`

issuesFound.title: .nav-toggle:focus-visible removes outline without visible replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle button loses its focus indicator, leaving keyboard users unable to see when it is focused.
issuesFound.fix:
Add a visible focus style.  
Line 906: `.nav-toggle:focus-visible { outline: none; outline-offset: 3px; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px var(--colour-focus-ring); }`

issuesFound.title: .filter-tabs button:focus-visible removes outline without visible replacement
issuesFound.explanation: Filter‑tab buttons lose their focus indicator, preventing users from perceiving which filter is active via keyboard.
issuesFound.fix:
Add a visible focus style.  
Line 1005: `.filter-tabs button:focus-visible { outline: none; outline-offset: 3px; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px var(--colour-focus-ring); }`

issuesFound.title: .pricing-toggle button:focus-visible removes outline without visible replacement

issuesFound.title: .table-wrapper:focus-visible removes outline without visible replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The scrollable table wrapper loses its focus outline, making it impossible for keyboard users to locate the focusable region.

issuesFound.title: .skip-link transition lacks prefers‑reduced‑motion guard
issuesFound.explanation: The skip‑link animates `transform` on focus/hover, but no `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` override disables it, violating WCAG 2.3.3 for users who prefer reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix:
Add a reduced‑motion override.  
Line 527: `transition: transform var(--transition-base);`  
Line 548‑549 (add inside the existing prefers‑reduced‑motion block):  
`@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .skip-link { transition: none; } }`

issuesFound.explanation: The hero image animates `transform` and `box‑shadow` on hover, but no reduced‑motion media query disables these animations, affecting users who request reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix:
Add a reduced‑motion override.  
Line 1273: `transition: transform var(--transition-slower), box-shadow var(--transition-slower);`  
Add inside the existing prefers‑reduced‑motion block (lines 1282‑1287):  
`@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transition: none; } .hero-image img:hover { transform: none; box-shadow: none; } }`

`@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .logo-list img { transition: none; } }`

issuesFound.fix: Line 306: `:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }`

issuesFound.fix: Line 311: `:focus:not(:focus-visible) { outline: 2px dotted var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 2px; }`

issuesFound.fix: Line 1320: `.form-group input[type="text"], .form-group input[type="email"], .form-group select, .form-group textarea { transition: border-color var(--transition-fast), box-shadow var(--transition-fast); } @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .form-group input[type="text"], .form-group input[type="email"], .form-group select, .form-group textarea { transition: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: Transitions without reduced‑motion guard on social‑link anchors
issuesFound.explanation: `.social-links a` defines a `transition` on `color` but does not include a reduced‑motion override.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1504: `.social-links a { transition: color var(--transition-fast); } @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .social-links a { transition: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: Transitions without reduced‑motion guard on social‑link images
issuesFound.explanation: `.social-links img` includes a `transition` on `opacity` without a reduced‑motion safeguard.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1510: `.social-links img { transition: opacity var(--transition-fast); } @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .social-links img { transition: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: Body text set below 16 px
issuesFound.explanation: The `html` selector sets `font-size: 12px;`, which is below the WCAG‑AA minimum readable size of 16 px for body text. Users with low vision may struggle to read content.
issuesFound.fix: Line 90:   font-size: 1rem; /* change to 16 px (or larger) */

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on generic :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The rule `:focus-visible { outline: none; … }` removes the native focus outline without providing an alternative visual cue, breaking WCAG 2.4.7 (focus visible). Keyboard‑only users lose the focus indicator.
issuesFound.fix: Line 306: :focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on :focus:not(:focus-visible)
issuesFound.explanation: `:focus:not(:focus-visible) { outline: none; }` strips the focus ring for mouse‑only interactions without a replacement, violating focus‑visibility requirements.
issuesFound.fix: Line 311: :focus:not(:focus-visible) { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 2px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on .header-inner > a:first-child:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The logo link’s focus style removes the outline without a visible replacement, breaking focus visibility.
issuesFound.fix: Line 511: .header-inner > a:first-child:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on .feature-card a:focus-visible

issuesFound.title: Transition without reduced‑motion guard on .skip-link
issuesFound.explanation: `.skip-link` defines a `transition: transform var(--transition-base);` but no `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` override disables or slows it, violating WCAG 2.3.3.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 277: .skip-link { transition: transform var(--transition-base); }
  Line 285: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .skip-link { transition: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Transition without reduced‑motion guard on .btn

Line 447: .btn { transition: background-color var(--transition-fast), border-color var(--transition-fast), color var(--transition-fast), box-shadow var(--transition-fast), transform var(--transition-fast); }
  Line 460: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn { transition: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Mobile‑navigation toggle button missing `aria‑expanded` (and `aria‑controls`)
issuesFound.explanation: The element referenced by `SELECTORS.navToggle` (the “hamburger” button) controls the off‑canvas navigation drawer, but the script never sets `aria‑expanded` or `aria‑controls` on it. Assistive technologies therefore cannot convey the drawer’s state to users, breaching WCAG 2.1 (ARIA required state) and the “ARIA: Best Practices – Accessible name and role are required” rule.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle button missing aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The element matched by SELECTORS.navToggle (the hamburger/menu button) toggles the mobile navigation drawer but never updates an aria‑expanded state. Screen‑reader users cannot know whether the menu is open or closed.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle button missing aria‑controls
issuesFound.explanation: The same .nav‑toggle button controls the visibility of the navigation drawer but does not expose an aria‑controls attribute linking it to the drawer element, leaving assistive technologies without a programmatic relationship.

issuesFound.title: IconButton missing accessible name in ThemeToggle
issuesFound.explanation: The IconButton component requires an `aria-label` to provide an accessible name for screen‑reader users. Without it the theme‑toggle button is announced as “button” with no description, preventing users from understanding its purpose.
issuesFound.fix: Line 562: `<IconButton aria-label="Toggle theme" onClick={() => setTheme(NEXT[theme])} className="theme-toggle">`

issuesFound.title: IconButton missing accessible name in MobileNav close button
issuesFound.explanation: The close button inside the mobile navigation uses IconButton without an `aria-label`. Screen‑reader users hear “button” with no context, making it impossible to know it closes the menu.
issuesFound.fix: Line 384: `<IconButton aria-label="Close navigation menu" onClick={onClose} className="mobile-nav__close">✕</IconButton>`

issuesFound.title: IconButton missing accessible name on carousel “previous” control
issuesFound.explanation: The previous‑slide button in the testimonials carousel is an icon‑only button lacking an `aria-label`. Assistive technologies cannot convey its function, blocking keyboard users from navigating the carousel.
issuesFound.fix: Line 952: `<IconButton aria-label="Previous testimonial" aria-controls={trackId} onClick={handlePrev} className="testimonials-track__prev">‹</IconButton>`

issuesFound.title: IconButton missing accessible name on carousel “next” control
issuesFound.explanation: The next‑slide button in the testimonials carousel is an icon‑only button without an `aria-label`, leaving its purpose undisclosed to screen‑reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 962: `<IconButton aria-label="Next testimonial" aria-controls={trackId} onClick={handleNext} className="testimonials-track__next">›</IconButton>`

issuesFound.title: MobileNav section toggle button missing `aria-expanded`
issuesFound.explanation: The button that expands/collapses a mobile navigation section lacks `aria-expanded`, so assistive technologies cannot convey its collapsed/expanded state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 345: `<button type="button" aria-controls={sectionId} aria-expanded={isExpanded} className="mobile-nav__link mobile-nav__link--trigger" onClick={() => setOpenSectionId(isExpanded ? null : item.href)}>`

issuesFound.title: Modal overlay `<div>` with `onClick` missing interactive role and keyboard support
issuesFound.explanation: The overlay that closes the modal is a non‑interactive `<div>` with an `onClick` handler but no `role="button"`, `tabIndex`, or keyboard handler. Keyboard‑only users cannot activate the overlay to dismiss the dialog.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1158: `<div role="button" tabIndex={0} onKeyDown={(e)=>{if(e.key===KEY.enter||e.key===KEY.space){e.preventDefault();onClose();}}} className="modal-overlay" aria-modal="true" onClick={onClose}>`

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation backdrop `<div>` with `onClick` missing interactive role and keyboard support
issuesFound.explanation: The backdrop that closes the mobile navigation is a plain `<div>` with an `onClick` handler but no `role="button"`, `tabIndex`, or keyboard handling, making it inaccessible to keyboard users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 306: `<div role="button" tabIndex={0} onKeyDown={(e)=>{if(e.key===KEY.enter||e.key===KEY.space){e.preventDefault();onClose();}}} className="mobile-nav-backdrop" onClick={onClose} />`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without visible alternative
issuesFound.explanation: The CSS removes the default focus outline with `outline: none` on `:focus:not(:focus-visible)` and does not provide a visible focus indicator for keyboard users who rely on `:focus` (e.g., older browsers or non-supporting AT). This fails WCAG 2.4.7 Focus Visible (Level AA).
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1001: Remove the rule `:focus:not(:focus-visible) { outline: none; }`
    Line 1005: Ensure the `:focus-visible` rule is the only focus style applied.

issuesFound.title: Missing prefers-reduced-motion override for hero image hover animation
issuesFound.explanation: The `.hero-image img:hover` transform and box-shadow animation lacks a `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` override, causing motion sickness for users who prefer reduced motion. This fails WCAG 2.3.3 Animation from Interactions (Level AA).

issuesFound.title: Missing prefers-reduced-motion override for feature card hover animation
issuesFound.explanation: The `.feature-card:hover` transform animation lacks a `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` override, causing motion sickness for users who prefer reduced motion. This fails WCAG 2.3.3 Animation from Interactions (Level AA).

issuesFound.title: Missing prefers-reduced-motion override for product card hover animation
issuesFound.explanation: The `.product-card:hover` transform animation lacks a `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` override, causing motion sickness for users who prefer reduced motion. This fails WCAG 2.3.3 Animation from Interactions (Level AA).

issuesFound.title: Missing prefers-reduced-motion override for pricing card hover animation
issuesFound.explanation: The `.pricing-card:hover` transform animation lacks a `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` override, causing motion sickness for users who prefer reduced motion. This fails WCAG 2.3.3 Animation from Interactions (Level AA).

issuesFound.title: Missing prefers-reduced-motion override for stat item hover animation
issuesFound.explanation: The `.stat-item:hover` transform animation lacks a `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` override, causing motion sickness for users who prefer reduced motion. This fails WCAG 2.3.3 Animation from Interactions (Level AA).

issuesFound.title: Missing prefers-reduced-motion override for FAQ item arrow animation
issuesFound.explanation: The `.faq-item dt button::after` transform animation lacks a `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` override, causing motion sickness for users who prefer reduced motion. This fails WCAG 2.3.3 Animation from Interactions (Level AA).

issuesFound.title: Body text font size below 16px
issuesFound.explanation: The `html` element sets `font-size: 12px`, which causes all rem-based font sizes to be smaller than the recommended minimum of 16px for body text. This reduces readability, especially for users with low vision. This fails WCAG 1.4.4 Resize Text (Level AA).
issuesFound.fix: Line 970: Replace `font-size: 12px;` with `font-size: 16px;`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed in forced-colors mode without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The `:focus-visible` rule removes the outline in forced-colors mode (`outline: none`) without providing an alternative focus indicator. This fails WCAG 2.4.7 Focus Visible (Level AA) in high contrast modes.

issuesFound.title: Skip link focus style may not meet 3:1 contrast in all themes
issuesFound.explanation: The `.skip-link:focus` outline uses `var(--colour-neutral-0)` (white) on a background that may also be light, failing to meet the 3:1 contrast requirement for focus indicators (WCAG 1.4.11 Non-Text Contrast). The contrast is not guaranteed in all color schemes.

issuesFound.title: Empty prefers-reduced-motion media query
issuesFound.explanation: The `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` block is empty, missing an opportunity to disable or slow down animations globally. While not a direct failure, this is a best practice gap.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without visible alternative
issuesFound.explanation: The CSS removes the default focus outline with `outline: none` on `:focus-visible` and `:focus:not(:focus-visible)` but does not provide a visible focus indicator that meets WCAG 2.4.11 (minimum 3:1 contrast, 2 CSS pixels thick, and at least as large as a 24×24 px area). Keyboard users may lose track of focus.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 438: `:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }`
    Line 442: `:focus:not(:focus-visible) { outline: none; }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator colour lacks sufficient contrast
issuesFound.explanation: The `--colour-focus-ring: #aaaaaa` has a contrast ratio of only 1.9:1 against the default white background (`--colour-neutral-0: #ffffff`). This fails WCAG 2.4.11 (minimum 3:1 contrast for focus indicators).
issuesFound.fix: Line 438: Replace `--colour-focus-ring: #aaaaaa;` with `--colour-focus-ring: #005fcc;` (or any colour with ≥ 3:1 contrast against the background).

issuesFound.title: Reduced motion media query is empty
issuesFound.explanation: The `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` block is present but empty. Users who prefer reduced motion will still see all animations and transitions, which can cause discomfort or seizures (WCAG 2.3.3).
issuesFound.fix: Line 448: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { *, *::before, *::after { animation-duration: 0.01ms !important; animation-iteration-count: 1 !important; transition-duration: 0.01ms !important; scroll-behavior: auto !important; } }`

issuesFound.title: Body font size below 16px
issuesFound.explanation: The `html` element sets `font-size: 12px` and the `body` inherits `font-size: var(--font-size-base)` which is `1rem` (12px). Text below 16px can be difficult to read for users with low vision (WCAG 1.4.4 Resize Text).
issuesFound.fix:
Line 104: `font-size: 16px;`
    Line 106: Remove `font-size: var(--font-size-base);` from the `body` rule (or update `--font-size-base` to `1rem` in `:root` if 16px is intended).

issuesFound.title: Outline removed without :focus-visible alternative
issuesFound.explanation: The CSS removes the default focus outline with `outline: none` on both `:focus-visible` and `:focus:not(:focus-visible)`. While `:focus-visible` is intended to provide a focus indicator, the current implementation does not ensure a visible focus indicator is present for keyboard users (WCAG 2.1.1 Keyboard).
issuesFound.fix:
Line 438: `:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }`
    Line 442: `:focus:not(:focus-visible) { outline: none; }`
    (Ensure `--colour-focus-ring` has sufficient contrast as noted in Issue 2.)

issuesFound.title: Skip link focus style lacks sufficient contrast
issuesFound.explanation: The skip link focus outline is `outline: 3px solid var(--colour-neutral-0);` where `--colour-neutral-0` is `#ffffff`. This has 1:1 contrast against the skip link background (`--colour-brand-primary: #5588bb`), failing WCAG 2.4.11 (minimum 3:1 contrast for focus indicators).
issuesFound.fix: Line 470: `outline: 3px solid var(--colour-brand-primary-hover);` (or any colour with ≥ 3:1 contrast against the skip link background).

issuesFound.title: Forced colors mode focus indicator removed
issuesFound.explanation: In forced colors mode, the focus indicator is removed with `outline: none` without providing an alternative. Users in high contrast modes may lose focus visibility (WCAG 2.4.11).
issuesFound.fix: Line 454: Remove the `outline: none;` rule or replace with a forced colors compatible focus style (e.g., `outline: 2px solid ButtonText;`).

issuesFound.title: Button active state transform may cause motion discomfort
issuesFound.explanation: The `.btn:active` rule applies a `transform: translateY(1px);` which creates motion. Users who prefer reduced motion may experience discomfort (WCAG 2.3.3).

issuesFound.explanation: The `.hero-image img:hover` rule applies a 3D transform and shadow animation. Users who prefer reduced motion may experience discomfort or distraction (WCAG 2.3.3).

issuesFound.title: Missing prefers-reduced-motion guard for transitions
issuesFound.explanation: Multiple CSS transitions and animations (e.g., hover effects, transforms) are defined without a `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` guard. Users who prefer reduced motion may experience vestibular discomfort or distraction.
issuesFound.fix:
Wrap all non-essential transitions and animations in a `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` block that disables or slows them. Example for each affected line:
    Line 101: `transition: background-color var(--transition-fast), border-color var(--transition-fast), color var(--transition-fast);`
    Line 102: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { transition: none; }`
    (Repeat this pattern for every transition/animation line listed above.)

issuesFound.title: Body font size below 16px
issuesFound.explanation: The `html` element sets `font-size: 12px`, which causes all `rem` units to be based on 12px. This results in body text smaller than 16px, reducing readability for users with low vision or cognitive disabilities.
issuesFound.fix: Line 150: `font-size: 16px;`

issuesFound.title: Missing keyboard support for non-native interactive element

This prevents keyboard users from opening/closing the mobile menu, violating WCAG 2.1.1 Keyboard.

issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle (line 10045) dynamically announces "Navigation menu opened" and "Navigation menu closed" but does not use an ARIA live region to announce these state changes to screen readers. This violates WCAG 4.1.3 Status Messages.

issuesFound.title: Missing focus management on modal open
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer (line 10045) is a modal dialog but does not programmatically move focus to the first focusable element inside the drawer when opened. This violates WCAG 2.4.3 Focus Order.

issuesFound.title: Missing focus management on modal close
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer (line 10045) does not return focus to the trigger button when closed. This violates WCAG 2.4.3 Focus Order.

issuesFound.title: Missing focus trap for modal dialog
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer (line 10045) is a modal dialog but does not implement a focus trap to constrain keyboard focus within the drawer. This violates WCAG 2.1.2 No Keyboard Trap.

issuesFound.title: Missing ARIA role for composite widget

issuesFound.title: Missing ARIA state for interactive element

issuesFound.title: Missing ARIA property for interactive element

issuesFound.title: Missing form label for input

issuesFound.title: Global focus indicator removed
issuesFound.explanation: The :focus-visible selector sets outline: none without defining a visible alternative (such as border or box-shadow). This removes the keyboard focus indicator for all elements that do not explicitly override it with a visible style, making keyboard navigation impossible to track.
issuesFound.fix: Line 335: :focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: Screen-reader-only class fails to hide content
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class sets position: static and clip: auto, which makes content visible on screen instead of hiding it visually while keeping it available to assistive technology. This breaks the mechanism for providing accessible labels or headings without visual clutter.
issuesFound.fix: Line 357: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Skip link remains off-screen when focused
issuesFound.explanation: The .skip-link element is translated off-screen using transform: translateY(-200%), but the :focus state does not reset this transform. Keyboard users will not see the skip link when tabbing to it, preventing them from bypassing navigation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 463: .skip-link:focus { transform: translateY(0); outline: 3px solid var(--colour-neutral-0); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Root font size below accessibility minimum
issuesFound.explanation: The html element font-size is set to 12px, which causes the base body text (1rem) to render at 12px. This is below the recommended 16px minimum for readability, creating a barrier for users with low vision.
issuesFound.fix: Line 165: html { font-size: 16px; scroll-behavior: smooth; text-size-adjust: 100%; -webkit-text-size-adjust: 100%; line-height: var(--line-height-normal); tab-size: 4; -moz-tab-size: 4; height: 100%; }

issuesFound.title: Links indistinguishable from text
issuesFound.explanation: The global a selector sets text-decoration: none without a global hover state that adds an underline. Inline links within body text may rely solely on color to be identified, which fails WCAG 1.4.1 Use of Color for users who cannot perceive color differences.
issuesFound.fix: Line 203: a { text-decoration: underline; }

issuesFound.title: Animation without reduced motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn selector defines transition properties with non-zero durations, but there is no corresponding @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override for this specific component to disable the animation. The global media query for reduced motion is empty and provides no protection.

issuesFound.title: Empty reduced motion media query
issuesFound.explanation: The @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block is empty. It fails to disable global animations and transitions (such as scroll-behavior or transitions on elements without specific overrides), violating WCAG 2.3.3 Animation from Interactions.
issuesFound.fix: Line 478: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { *, *::before, *::after { animation-duration: 0.01ms !important; animation-iteration-count: 1 !important; transition-duration: 0.01ms !important; scroll-behavior: auto !important; } }

issuesFound.title: Root Font Size Below Readability Threshold
issuesFound.explanation: The root element font-size is set to 12px, which makes the base font size (1rem) smaller than the recommended 16px minimum for body text. This degrades readability for users with low vision or cognitive disabilities and may prevent text from scaling correctly when users increase browser zoom.
issuesFound.fix: Line 53: html { font-size: 100%; }

issuesFound.title: Screen Reader Only Class Exposes Content Visually
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static and clip: auto, which fails to visually hide content intended only for screen readers. This clutters the visual interface for sighted users and violates the expectation that this utility class hides elements while keeping them accessible to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line 573: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Global Focus Indicator Removed Without Replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector sets outline: none without defining a visible alternative (such as box-shadow or border) in the same rule block. This removes the keyboard focus indicator for all elements unless specifically overridden, making it impossible for keyboard-only users to track their location on the page.
issuesFound.fix: Line 533: :focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 2px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: Multiple Components Remove Focus Indicators
issuesFound.explanation: Multiple component-specific :focus-visible selectors explicitly set outline: none without providing a visible focus style (like box-shadow or border) in their respective blocks. This reinforces the global failure and ensures that buttons, links, and form controls in these sections remain invisible to keyboard users when focused.

issuesFound.title: Missing Reduced Motion Overrides for Transitions
issuesFound.explanation: Several elements define CSS transitions (skip-link, logo-list img) but lack a corresponding @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override to disable them. Additionally, the global reduced motion media query at line 1363 is empty, failing to act as a safety net. This can cause discomfort or vestibular issues for users who prefer reduced motion.

issuesFound.title: Root font size set below 16px
issuesFound.explanation: The html element font-size is set to 12px, which makes the base 1rem equal to 12px. This significantly reduces text readability for users with low vision and contradicts the standard 16px browser default recommended for accessibility.
issuesFound.fix: Line 159: font-size: 100%;

issuesFound.title: Global focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The :focus-visible selector sets outline: none but does not define a visible alternative such as a border or box-shadow. This removes the keyboard focus indicator for all elements relying on this global rule, making navigation impossible for keyboard-only users.

issuesFound.title: Button focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible selector sets outline: none without providing a visible focus style like a border or box-shadow. This overrides any global focus style and hides the focus state for all buttons using this class.

issuesFound.title: Code text fails color contrast
issuesFound.explanation: The code element uses a light purple text color (#bb88ee) on a light gray background (#f1f5f9). The contrast ratio is approximately 2.5:1, which fails the WCAG AA requirement of 4.5:1 for normal text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 426: color: var(--colour-brand-secondary-hover);

issuesFound.title: Reduced motion media query is empty
issuesFound.explanation: The @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block is empty. It fails to disable transitions defined elsewhere (e.g., .skip-link), causing motion to persist for users who have requested reduced motion.

issuesFound.title: Hero image transition not disabled for reduced motion
issuesFound.explanation: The media query for reduced motion targets .hero-image img but the rule block is empty. The transform and box-shadow transitions defined on this element will still play for users who prefer reduced motion.

issuesFound.title: Logo list image transition not disabled for reduced motion
issuesFound.explanation: The reduced motion media query disables animation on .logo-list but misses the transition defined on .logo-list img. The filter transition on the images will still occur for users who prefer reduced motion.

fpTitles: Hero image transition not disabled for reduced motion
Logo list image transition not disabled for reduced motion

issuesFound.title: Focus trap event listeners never attach due to undefined SUPPORTS.dom property
issuesFound.explanation: The createFocusTrap function checks `if (SUPPORTS.dom)` before attaching keydown and focusin event listeners, but SUPPORTS.dom is not defined anywhere in the SUPPORTS object (lines 352-419). This condition always evaluates to false, meaning the focus trap never actually constrains keyboard navigation. Users cannot be confined to modal dialogs, violating WCAG 2.1.1 Keyboard.

issuesFound.title: Filter chip updates not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The activeFilterChips.render() function dynamically updates the filter chip list when filters change, but no ARIA live region announcement is made. Screen reader users are not notified when chips are added or removed, degrading the experience for users relying on assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2329: liveRegion.announce(`${label} filter ${value ? 'removed' : 'applied'}`);

issuesFound.title: Search suggestion list content changes not announced beyond count
issuesFound.explanation: The suggestionListRenderer.render() function updates the combobox listbox content dynamically, but only the result count is announced via _statusEl. The actual suggestion items changing is not announced, so screen reader users know results exist but not that the suggestion list content has updated.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1525: suggestionListRenderer.render(results, norm);
  Line 1526: liveRegion.announce(`${results.length} search suggestions available`);

issuesFound.title: Non-button FAQ trigger elements missing keyboard activation handler
issuesFound.explanation: The faqRegistry.parse() function assigns role="button" and tabindex="0" to trigger elements that are not native <button> or <summary> elements (lines 2847-2854), making them keyboard-focusable custom controls. However, no keydown handler is attached to these elements to activate them with Enter or Space keys. The _onContainerClick handler only listens for click events, which do not fire for keyboard activation on non-button elements. This violates WCAG 2.1.1 (Keyboard) as keyboard users cannot activate these FAQ triggers.

issuesFound.title: FAQ search highlighting changes visible content without live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The highlightFaqTerms() function (lines 2740-2758) modifies the visible question text by injecting <mark> elements via innerHTML to highlight search matches. This changes visible page state but no ARIA live region announcement is made to inform screen reader users that highlighting has been applied. While the filter results count is announced separately, the actual content modification goes unannounced, violating WCAG 4.1.3 (Status Messages).
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2758: After updating innerHTML, add live region announcement:
    liveRegion.announce('Search terms highlighted in questions');

issuesFound.title: Search results highlighting changes visible content without live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The searchResultsHighlighter.highlight() function (lines 3345-3378) uses TreeWalker to find text nodes and replaces matching content with <mark> elements via DOM manipulation. This visibly changes the search results page content but provides no ARIA live region announcement to inform screen reader users that highlighting has been applied. Users relying on assistive technology are not notified of this state change, violating WCAG 4.1.3 (Status Messages).
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3378: After highlighting completes, add live region announcement:
    liveRegion.announce('Search terms highlighted in results');

issuesFound.title: replaceWith utility can inject HTML without live region consideration
issuesFound.explanation: The replaceWith() function (lines 1089-1099) uses insertAdjacentHTML() to inject arbitrary HTML strings into the DOM when the replacement parameter is a string. This can change visible page state without any mechanism to announce the change to screen reader users. The function is a general utility used throughout the codebase for dynamic content updates, and lacks any integration with ARIA live regions, potentially violating WCAG 4.1.3 (Status Messages) when used for visible content changes.

issuesFound.title: Non-native interactive element missing keyboard handler
issuesFound.explanation: The file upload zone element is given role="button" and tabindex="0" to make it keyboard-focusable, but the keydown handler references KEYS.enter and KEYS.space which do not exist in the KEYS constant object (which defines ENTER and SPACE in uppercase). This means keyboard users pressing Enter or Space will not activate the file picker, blocking access for keyboard-only users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3787: if (e.key === KEYS.ENTER || e.key === KEYS.SPACE) {

issuesFound.title: Screen-reader-only class incorrectly styled as visible

issuesFound.title: CSS transitions not disabled for prefers-reduced-motion
issuesFound.explanation: Multiple selectors define CSS transitions with non-zero durations without a @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override that disables or slows the animation. Users who have enabled reduced motion preferences will still experience animations, which can cause vestibular disorders or discomfort.

issuesFound.title: Global :focus-visible removes focus outline without alternative
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible rule sets outline: none without defining an alternative visible focus indicator such as border or box-shadow. This removes the focus ring for all keyboard-focused elements, making navigation impossible for sighted keyboard users.

issuesFound.title: Forced colors mode focus indicator removed without alternative
issuesFound.explanation: In forced-colors mode, the focus indicator is removed with outline: none and no alternative visual indicator is provided. Users relying on high contrast themes will be unable to see which element has focus.

issuesFound.title: Submenu link focus indicator missing
issuesFound.explanation: Submenu links remove the focus outline in the :focus-visible state without providing an alternative visual indicator such as border or box-shadow.

issuesFound.title: Search button focus indicator missing
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button removes the focus outline without defining an alternative visible indicator, leaving keyboard users without focus feedback.

issuesFound.title: Account navigation link focus indicator missing

issuesFound.title: Feature card link focus indicator missing

issuesFound.title: Pricing toggle button focus indicator missing

issuesFound.title: Table wrapper focus indicator missing

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion button focus indicator missing

issuesFound.title: Step number text contrast on gradient insufficient

issuesFound.explanation: Setting root font-size to 12px results in body text of 12px (1rem), which is below the 16px recommended for readability and fails to respect user browser preferences adequately.

issuesFound.explanation: Numerous CSS transitions exist without a global @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override to disable or reduce motion, which can cause vestibular disorders for sensitive users.

issuesFound.explanation: The search input's label is hidden using display: none, which removes it from the accessibility tree, leaving the search input without an accessible name for screen reader users.
