issuesFound.title: Base font size below 16px
issuesFound.explanation: The html element sets font-size to 12px, which is below the recommended 16px (1rem) for body text. This creates readability barriers for users with low vision and makes it harder for users to resize text effectively using browser zoom controls.
issuesFound.fix: Line 118: `font-size: 16px;` or remove the declaration to use the browser default (typically 16px).

issuesFound.title: Paragraph line height insufficient
issuesFound.explanation: The p selector sets line-height to 1, which is well below the WCAG recommended minimum of 1.5. This dense spacing significantly reduces readability for users with dyslexia or cognitive disabilities and increases eye strain.
issuesFound.fix: Line 166: `line-height: var(--line-height-normal);` (or 1.5)

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without visible replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The :focus-visible pseudo-class sets outline: none without providing an alternative visible focus indicator (such as a border, box-shadow, or background change). This removes the focus ring entirely, making it impossible for keyboard users to see which element has focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 344: Add a visible replacement such as `outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring);` or `box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px var(--colour-focus-ring);` in addition to the existing outline-offset.

issuesFound.title: Screen-reader-only class fails to hide content visually
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static, width: auto, height: auto, and overflow: visible, which fails to visually hide the content. This makes screen-reader text visible to sighted users while also potentially breaking the layout. The class should use absolute positioning, 1px dimensions, and clipping to hide content visually while keeping it in the accessibility tree.

issuesFound.title: Small button target size below 24×24px
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class sets min-height to 20px, which is below the WCAG 2.5.8 Target Size (Minimum) requirement of 24×24 pixels. This makes the button difficult or impossible for users with motor impairments to activate reliably.
issuesFound.fix: Line 621: `min-height: 24px;` (and ensure min-width is also at least 24px if applicable)

issuesFound.title: Icon-only button target size below 24×24px
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-icon class sets both min-width and min-height to 20px, failing the WCAG 2.5.8 minimum target size requirement of 24×24 pixels. Icon-only buttons are critical interactive elements that require sufficient touch target size.
issuesFound.fix: Lines 631-632: `min-width: 24px;` and `min-height: 24px;`

issuesFound.title: Checkbox input target size below 24×24px
issuesFound.explanation: The checkbox input sets width and height to 20px, which is below the recommended 24×24px target size for pointer inputs. While native checkboxes have some exceptions, explicitly sizing them below 24px creates barriers for users with tremors or limited fine motor control.
issuesFound.fix: Lines 1021-1023: `width: 24px; height: 24px; min-height: 24px;`

issuesFound.title: Search form label removed from accessibility tree
issuesFound.explanation: The selector #site-search label.sr-only applies display: none, which removes the label from the accessibility tree entirely. Screen reader users will not hear the label, and the search input will lack an accessible name unless redundant aria-label attributes exist in the HTML (which cannot be verified from this CSS file).
issuesFound.fix: Line 761-762: Remove `display: none;` and ensure the label uses proper visually-hidden styles (position: absolute, clip: rect, etc.) instead.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion trigger missing aria-expanded="true" when opening
issuesFound.explanation: The `open()` function in faqAccordion adds CSS classes and removes the hidden attribute from the panel, but never sets `aria-expanded="true"` on the trigger button. Although the `close()` function sets `aria-expanded="false"`, the open state is never communicated to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 6245: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `item.open = true;` in the `open()` function

issuesFound.title: Toast notification has contradictory ARIA live region
issuesFound.explanation: The toast element is created with `role="alert"` (which implies `aria-live="assertive"` and `aria-atomic="true"`) but explicitly sets `[ARIA.LIVE]: type === 'error' ? 'assertive' : 'polite'`. When the toast is not an error, this creates a contradiction between the assertive nature of role="alert" and the polite live region setting.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2415: Remove `[ARIA.LIVE]: type === 'error' ? 'assertive' : 'polite',` and `[ARIA.ATOMIC]: 'true',` from the createElement call, keeping only `role: 'alert'`

issuesFound.title: Wizard previous button has empty aria-label when disabled
issuesFound.explanation: In `_updateNavButtons()`, when `isFirst` is true, the code sets `_prevBtn.setAttribute('aria-label', isFirst ? '' : ...)` which results in an empty aria-label. Empty aria-label attributes prevent screen readers from announcing the button's purpose.
issuesFound.fix: Line 7240: Change to `_prevBtn.setAttribute('aria-label', isFirst ? 'Previous step (disabled)' : `Back to ${_steps[_current - 1].title}`);`

fpTitles: Wizard previous button has empty aria-label when disabled

issuesFound.title: Toast notification auto-dismisses without pause mechanism
issuesFound.explanation: The toastManager uses setTimeout to automatically dismiss notifications after a fixed duration (default 4000ms). There is no mechanism to pause, stop, or extend this timer when the user hovers over or focuses the toast. This violates WCAG 2.2.1 (Timing Adjustable) as users with reading disabilities may not have sufficient time to read the content before it disappears.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer missing focus trap implementation
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav module declares a `_trap` variable for focus trapping but never initializes it with createFocusTrap(). When the mobile menu opens, focus is not constrained to the drawer, allowing keyboard users to tab behind the modal overlay to the underlying page content. This violates WCAG 2.4.3 (Focus Order) and 2.4.7 (Focus Visible).

issuesFound.title: Wizard progress bar missing aria-valuemin and aria-valuemax
issuesFound.explanation: The multiStepWizard updates the progress bar's aria-valuenow and aria-valuetext attributes, but does not ensure aria-valuemin and aria-valuemax are set. Without these attributes, screen readers cannot accurately convey the progress bar's range to users.

issuesFound.title: Search suggestion list updates without ensuring accessible name for dynamic options
issuesFound.explanation: The suggestionListRenderer uses innerHTML to inject new list items into the listbox. While the container has role="listbox", individual items are created with role="option" but rely on the inner text content for their accessible name. If the highlighted text contains markup (mark elements), the accessible name calculation might be affected, and more importantly, the dynamic injection of content requires that the status region announce the count of results to ensure screen reader users are aware the list has updated.

issuesFound.title: File upload zone missing aria-describedby for file type restrictions
issuesFound.explanation: The fileUploadField creates an upload zone with role="button" and tabindex="0", but does not associate it with a description of allowed file types or size limits using aria-describedby. Users may not know what file types are accepted until after attempting to upload.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion triggers missing aria-expanded initial state synchronization
issuesFound.explanation: In faqAccordion.init, the aria-expanded attribute is set based on the item.open property, but for items that are initially closed (the default), the code sets aria-expanded="false" explicitly. However, if the HTML already has aria-expanded="true" on an item (data-faq-open), the code correctly handles it, but for dynamically added items or items without the attribute, the initial state might not be synchronized if the panel is visible but aria-expanded is not set.

issuesFound.title: Contact form error summary missing aria-live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When the contact form validation fails, the error summary is created and focused, but if the focus management fails or the user is using a screen reader that doesn't announce focused alerts, the error message might not be communicated. The error summary should have aria-live="assertive" to ensure immediate announcement.

issuesFound.title: Theme switcher toggle missing aria-checked for role="switch"
issuesFound.explanation: In themeSwitcher, when the toggle is not a native button and role="switch" is applied, the code sets aria-checked based on the theme, but for the system preference option, the checked state might not be accurately reflected if the resolved theme differs from the preference.

issuesFound.title: Media player controller missing aria-label for custom controls
issuesFound.explanation: The mediaPlayerController binds keyboard shortcuts to media elements, but if the media controls are custom (not native), they may not have accessible labels. The code assumes native controls or that the media element itself has a label, but doesn't ensure the media element has an aria-label or title describing the content.

fpTitles: Toast notification auto-dismisses without pause mechanism
Wizard progress bar missing aria-valuemin and aria-valuemax
File upload zone missing aria-describedby for file type restrictions
Media player controller missing aria-label for custom controls

issuesFound.title: Mobile Navigation Drawer Missing Focus Trap
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav` module declares `_trap` variable to hold a focus trap instance but never initializes it with `createFocusTrap()`. When the mobile menu opens, the code checks `if (_trap && _trap.isActive())` but `_trap` is always null, so focus is not trapped within the drawer. Keyboard users can tab out of the modal drawer into the background page, violating WCAG 2.4.3 (Focus Order) and 2.4.7 (Focus Visible).
issuesFound.fix: Line 2254-2256: Initialize the focus trap before opening. Add `_trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer, { returnFocus: _trigger });` before the open check, and update the condition to properly activate it.

issuesFound.title: Contact Form Personal Data Inputs Missing Autocomplete
issuesFound.explanation: The contact form handles personal data inputs (full_name, email) identified by selectors `[name="full_name"]` and `[name="email"]`, but the code does not programmatically add `autocomplete` attributes (e.g., "name", "email") to these fields. This prevents browsers and assistive technologies from programmatically identifying the input purpose, violating WCAG 2.2 Success Criterion 1.3.5 (Identify Input Purpose).
issuesFound.fix:
Line 4020-4025: After selecting each field, add autocomplete attributes based on the field name:
  Line 4021: `if (field.name === 'full_name') field.setAttribute('autocomplete', 'name');`
  Line 4023: `if (field.name === 'email') field.setAttribute('autocomplete', 'email');`

issuesFound.title: Newsletter Email Input Missing Autocomplete
issuesFound.explanation: The newsletter form selects the email input but does not ensure it has an `autocomplete="email"` attribute. Since this is a personal data input field for email subscription, it must have the autocomplete attribute to meet WCAG 2.2 Success Criterion 1.3.5.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3846-3847: After selecting `_emailEl`, add:
  Line 3847: `if (_emailEl && !_emailEl.getAttribute('autocomplete')) _emailEl.setAttribute('autocomplete', 'email');`

fpTitles: Contact Form Personal Data Inputs Missing Autocomplete
Newsletter Email Input Missing Autocomplete

issuesFound.title: ThemeToggle IconButton missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The IconButton component requires an aria-label prop to provide an accessible name for screen reader users, but the ThemeToggle component renders it without this required attribute. This results in a button that is announced only as "button" without indicating its purpose.

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel previous button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The previous slide button in the carousel uses IconButton without the required aria-label prop. The button contains only the character "‹" which is not descriptive enough for screen reader users to understand it navigates to the previous testimonial.

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel next button missing accessible name

This breaks the programmatic relationship between the label text and the form control, making it difficult for screen reader users to understand what the input is for.

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm email input broken aria-describedby reference

This creates a broken reference that causes screen readers to not announce error messages.

issuesFound.title: ContactForm name input broken aria-describedby reference

issuesFound.title: ContactForm email input broken aria-describedby reference

issuesFound.fix: Line 1936: Remove aria-describedby attribute or ensure FormGroup receives errorId="contact-email-error".

issuesFound.title: ContactForm subject input broken aria-describedby reference

issuesFound.title: ContactForm message textarea broken aria-describedby reference

issuesFound.fix: Line 1968: Remove 'contact-message-error' from aria-describedby or ensure FormGroup receives errorId="contact-message-error".

issuesFound.title: MobileNav section trigger missing aria-expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: The section toggle button in the mobile navigation has aria-controls but lacks aria-expanded to communicate whether the subsection is expanded or collapsed. This prevents screen reader users from understanding the current state.

issuesFound.title: BillingToggle monthly button missing aria-pressed

issuesFound.title: BillingToggle annual button missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The annual billing button acts as a toggle button indicating the current billing period selection, but lacks aria-pressed to convey its selected state to screen reader users. The visual styling alone does not communicate the pressed state programmatically.

issuesFound.title: Modal overlay has incorrect aria-modal attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The modal overlay div has aria-modal="true", but aria-modal should only be applied to the dialog element itself, not the backdrop. This incorrectly marks the backdrop as modal content, potentially confusing screen readers about which element represents the modal dialog.

issuesFound.title: createFocusTrap references undefined SUPPORTS.dom property
issuesFound.explanation: The createFocusTrap function checks `if (SUPPORTS.dom)` before adding keyboard and focus event listeners, but `SUPPORTS.dom` is never defined in the SUPPORTS object. This causes a runtime error (undefined property access) which prevents the focus trap from working at all. Focus traps are essential for modal dialogs and off-canvas menus to contain keyboard focus for screen reader and keyboard-only users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2630: `if (typeof document !== 'undefined') {` and Line 2631: `document.addEventListener('keydown',  _onKeydown,  true);`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open does not set aria-expanded on trigger button
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer opens, the `open()` function adds CSS classes and locks scroll but never sets `aria-expanded="true"` on the trigger button (`_trigger`). Screen reader users cannot tell whether the menu is open or closed because the trigger's state is not communicated. The close function also does not set `aria-expanded="false"`.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2861: `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` and add after Line 2888 in close function: `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: Pricing toggle with role="switch" missing aria-checked attribute
issuesFound.explanation: When the pricing toggle element is not a native `<button>`, the code assigns `role="switch"` and `tabindex="0"`, but never sets `aria-checked`. Elements with `role="switch"` must have `aria-checked="true"` or `aria-checked="false"` to communicate their state to screen reader users. The `_syncToggleState()` function only toggles a CSS class on `_toggleTrack`, not the required `aria-checked` on `_toggleEl`.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4499: `setAttr(_toggleEl, ARIA.CHECKED, String(isYear));` and Line 4501: `_toggleEl.setAttribute('aria-checked', String(isYear));`

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion triggers with role="button" lack Enter/Space keyboard handlers
issuesFound.explanation: In `faqRegistry.parse()`, non-button elements (like `<div>` or `<span>`) are given `role="button"` and `tabindex="0"` to make them focusable. However, the keyboard handler `_onKeydown()` only processes arrow keys, Home, and End for navigation between items—it does not handle Enter or Space to toggle the current item. Native `<button>` elements activate on Enter/Space automatically, but elements with `role="button"` require explicit keyboard event handlers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4247: `case KEYS.ENTER:` and Line 4248: `case KEYS.SPACE:` and Line 4249: `e.preventDefault(); toggle(item); break;`

issuesFound.title: Progress bar in multiStepWizard missing required aria-valuemin and aria-valuemax
issuesFound.explanation: The progress bar element is assigned `role="progressbar"` implicitly through the `data-wizard-progress` selector, and `aria-valuenow` and `aria-valuetext` are set dynamically. However, `aria-valuemin` and `aria-valuemax` are never set. Per WCAG and ARIA specifications, elements with `role="progressbar"` must have `aria-valuemin`, `aria-valuemax`, and `aria-valuenow` to be accessible to screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 5108: `_progressBar.setAttribute('aria-valuemin', '0');` and Line 5109: `_progressBar.setAttribute('aria-valuemax', '100');`

issuesFound.title: Product filter tabs do not communicate active state via aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The `_syncTabAriaStates()` function only toggles a CSS class (`CSS.active`) on filter tab buttons to indicate the active category. Screen reader users cannot perceive this visual state change. Filter buttons that act as toggles should use `aria-pressed="true"` for the active button and `aria-pressed="false"` for inactive buttons to communicate state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3875: `setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, String(isActive));`

issuesFound.title: Live region assertive container uses role="status" instead of role="alert"
issuesFound.explanation: The `makeRegion()` function creates both polite and assertive live regions with `role="status"`. For assertive announcements (which interrupt screen reader speech), `role="alert"` is more appropriate as it has implicit `aria-live="assertive"` and is specifically designed for important, time-sensitive notifications. Using `role="status"` for assertive content may cause inconsistent screen reader behavior.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2339: `role: politeness === 'assertive' ? 'alert' : 'status',`

fpTitles: FAQ accordion triggers with role="button" lack Enter/Space keyboard handlers
Progress bar in multiStepWizard missing required aria-valuemin and aria-valuemax

issuesFound.title: Focus trap event listeners never attached due to undefined property
issuesFound.explanation: The code checks `if (SUPPORTS.dom)` before adding keyboard and focus event listeners for the focus trap. However, `SUPPORTS.dom` is never defined in the SUPPORTS object, so it evaluates to undefined (falsy). This means the focus trap's critical event listeners are never attached, completely breaking focus containment for modals and dialogs. Keyboard users can tab outside the trap, violating WCAG 2.4.3 Focus Order and 2.1.2 No Keyboard Trap.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2850: `    if (true) {` OR add `dom: true` to the SUPPORTS object around line 950.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu trigger missing aria-expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu's `register()` function sets `aria-haspopup` and `aria-controls` on trigger buttons, but never sets `aria-expanded`. The `_openInstance()` and `_closeInstance()` functions also fail to toggle `aria-expanded`. Screen reader users cannot tell whether a dropdown is open or closed, violating WCAG 4.1.2 Name, Role, Value.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3225: `    if (!trigger.hasAttribute('aria-haspopup')) setAttr(trigger, 'aria-haspopup', 'true');` should be followed by `    if (!trigger.hasAttribute('aria-expanded')) setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` and Line 3248: `    setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` should be added inside `_openInstance`, and Line 3260: `    setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` should be added inside `_closeInstance`.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger missing aria-expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation `open()` and `close()` functions toggle CSS classes and emit events but never set `aria-expanded` on the trigger button (`_trigger`). Screen reader users have no indication of whether the navigation drawer is open or closed, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3127: `    setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` should be added inside `open()`, and Line 3143: `    setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` should be added inside `close()`.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open function fails to set aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The `open()` function in faqAccordion sets `item.open = true`, adds CSS classes, and animates the panel, but never sets `aria-expanded="true"` on the trigger button. The `close()` function correctly sets `aria-expanded="false"`, creating an inconsistent state where opening an item leaves the trigger incorrectly reporting it as collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 5312: `    setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` should be added immediately after `item.open = true;`.

issuesFound.title: Toast notification uses conflicting role="alert" with aria-live="polite"
issuesFound.explanation: When creating toast notifications, the code sets `role="alert"` (which implicitly has `aria-live="assertive"`) but also sets `aria-live="polite"` for non-error toasts. The `role="alert"` overrides the `aria-live="polite"` attribute, making all toasts assertive regardless of type. This causes excessive interruptions for screen reader users, violating WCAG 4.1.3 Status Messages.

issuesFound.title: Form validation fails to set aria-invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: The `markInvalid()` function in srUtils adds `aria-describedby` to link the field to its error message, but never sets `aria-invalid="true"` on the field. Screen readers cannot programmatically determine that the field has failed validation, violating WCAG 4.1.2 and 3.3.1 Error Identification.

issuesFound.title: Carousel slides missing role="group" for proper semantics
issuesFound.explanation: Each slide in the testimonials carousel receives `aria-roledescription="slide"` and `aria-label`, but lacks a base role. The `aria-roledescription` attribute requires a base role to be meaningful. Without `role="group"`, assistive technologies may not properly convey the slide's semantic purpose.

issuesFound.title: Wizard progress bar missing role="progressbar" and aria attributes
issuesFound.explanation: The wizard's progress bar element (`_progressBar`) is updated with `aria-valuenow` and `aria-valuetext`, but the code never ensures it has `role="progressbar"`, `aria-valuemin`, or `aria-valuemax`. Without these attributes, screen readers cannot properly announce progress as users move through the wizard steps.

fpTitles: Toast notification uses conflicting role="alert" with aria-live="polite"
Carousel slides missing role="group" for proper semantics

issuesFound.title: Pricing period change not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When users switch between monthly and annual billing periods, the prices update visually but screen reader users receive no notification. The STRINGS object contains announcement strings (STRINGS.announce.pricingMonthly, STRINGS.announce.pricingAnnual) but they are never used. This violates WCAG 4.1.3 (Status Messages) because a status message (billing period change) is not programmatically determinable through live region.

issuesFound.title: Product filter change not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When users apply a product category filter, the visible products change but screen reader users receive no notification. The STRINGS object contains announcement strings (STRINGS.announce.filterApplied, STRINGS.announce.filterAll) but they are never used. This violates WCAG 4.1.3 (Status Messages) because a status message (filter applied) is not programmatically determinable through live region.

issuesFound.title: FormGroup label missing htmlFor association
issuesFound.explanation: The FormGroup component accepts an htmlFor prop but never applies it to the label element. This breaks the programmatic association between labels and form controls, making it difficult for screen reader users to understand which label belongs to which input.
issuesFound.fix: Line 735: <label htmlFor={htmlFor} className="form-label">

issuesFound.title: ThemeToggle missing required aria-label
issuesFound.explanation: The ThemeToggle component uses IconButton but does not pass the required aria-label prop. Icon-only buttons must have an accessible name to be usable by screen reader users. The LABELS object exists but is never passed to the IconButton.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1115: <IconButton aria-label={LABELS[theme]} onClick={() => setTheme(NEXT[theme])} className="theme-toggle">

issuesFound.title: Modal has aria-modal on wrong element
issuesFound.explanation: The aria-modal="true" attribute is placed on the overlay div instead of the dialog element. Per ARIA specifications, aria-modal must be on the same element as role="dialog" to properly indicate modality to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1842: Remove aria-modal="true" from the overlay div.
  Fix: Line 1847: Add aria-modal="true" to the dialog div alongside role="dialog".

issuesFound.title: StatItem not exposing accessible value label
issuesFound.explanation: The stat values like "40,000+" and "99.9%" are displayed visually but screen readers may announce them unclearly. The Stat interface includes valueLabel for accessibility but it is never used in the rendering.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1203:

{stat.value}

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm TextInput has broken aria-describedby reference
issuesFound.explanation: The email input's aria-describedby references 'newsletter-email-error' when there is an error, but no element with that ID exists in the document. This breaks the association between the input and its error message for screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: The FormGroup component needs to render the ErrorMessage when there's an error. The calling code passes an 'error' prop but FormGroup expects 'errorId' and 'errorMessage'. Either fix FormGroup to accept an error prop, or update NewsletterForm to pass errorId and errorMessage correctly.

issuesFound.title: ContactForm inputs have broken aria-describedby references
issuesFound.explanation: Multiple inputs (contact-name, contact-email, contact-subject, contact-message) have aria-describedby references to error IDs that do not exist in the document. Screen reader users cannot access the error messages associated with each field.
issuesFound.fix: The FormGroup component needs to properly render error messages. Either fix FormGroup to accept and render error prop, or pass errorId and errorMessage props correctly for each field.

issuesFound.title: HeroSection section missing aria-labelledby
issuesFound.explanation: The hero section element has no aria-labelledby to associate it with its heading. While the h1 has id="hero-heading", the section does not reference it, making landmark navigation less descriptive for screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1141: <section className="hero-section" aria-labelledby="hero-heading">

issuesFound.title: StatsBar section missing aria-labelledby
issuesFound.explanation: The stats section has a visually hidden h2 with id="stats-heading" but the section element does not reference it via aria-labelledby. This reduces the descriptiveness of the landmark for screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1196: <section className="stats-bar" aria-labelledby="stats-heading">

issuesFound.title: SkipLinks nav missing aria-label
issuesFound.explanation: The SkipLinks component renders a nav element without an accessible name. When multiple nav elements exist on a page, each should have a distinguishing label to help screen reader users navigate between them.
issuesFound.fix: Line 681: <nav aria-label="Skip links">

issuesFound.title: ThemeToggle IconButton missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The IconButton component requires an aria-label prop for accessible naming, but ThemeToggle does not provide one. Icon-only buttons without accessible names are completely inaccessible to screen reader users who cannot see the icon.

issuesFound.title: FormGroup label missing htmlFor association
issuesFound.explanation: The FormGroup component receives an htmlFor prop but does not apply it to the label element. Without the htmlFor attribute, the label is not programmatically associated with its input, making it harder for screen reader users to understand form field purposes and preventing click-to-focus functionality.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 644:
```tsx
      <label htmlFor={htmlFor} className="form-label">
```

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm email input aria-describedby references non-existent ID
issuesFound.explanation: The email input's aria-describedby attribute references 'newsletter-email-error' which does not exist in the DOM because errorId is not passed to FormGroup and errorMessage is passed incorrectly as 'error'. Screen readers cannot announce validation errors for this field.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1708-1720:
```tsx
          <FormGroup
            label="Email address"
            htmlFor="newsletter-email"
            errorId="newsletter-email-error"
            errorMessage={email.state.touched ? email.state.error : undefined}
          >
            <TextInput
              id="newsletter-email"
              type="email"
              name="email"
              placeholder="you@company.com"
              autoComplete="email"
              value={email.state.value}
              onChange={(ev) => email.onChange(ev.target.value)}
              onBlur={email.onBlur}
              required
              invalid={email.state.touched && !!email.state.error}
              aria-describedby={email.state.touched && email.state.error ? 'newsletter-email-error' : undefined}
            />
          </FormGroup>
```

issuesFound.fix:

```tsx
          <FormGroup
            label="Full name"
            htmlFor="contact-name"
            required
            errorId="contact-name-error"

            />
          </FormGroup>
```

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm error message not rendered
issuesFound.explanation: The ErrorMessage component expects a 'message' prop but receives children instead. Since message is undefined, the component returns null and the error is never displayed. Users cannot perceive submission errors.

issuesFound.title: ContactForm error message not rendered
issuesFound.explanation: The ErrorMessage component expects a 'message' prop but receives children instead. Since message is undefined, the component returns null and the error is never displayed. Users cannot perceive submission errors.

issuesFound.title: Modal aria-modal placed on wrong element
issuesFound.explanation: The aria-modal="true" attribute is placed on the overlay div while role="dialog" is on the inner dialog div. According to ARIA specifications, aria-modal must be on the same element as role="dialog". This mismatch may cause screen readers to incorrectly handle the modal.

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel hidden slides missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: Non-visible carousel slides lack aria-hidden="true". Screen reader users may navigate to off-screen content using touch exploration or object navigation, causing confusion about which testimonial is currently displayed.

issuesFound.title: StatItem value lacks accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The stat value (e.g., "40,000+") is displayed without an accessible name for screen readers. While the visual presentation is clear, screen reader users may hear ambiguous pronunciation of formatted numbers. The valueLabel property exists in the Stat interface but is not used.

issuesFound.title: NavDropdown chevron missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The chevron span containing the arrow character (▲/▼) is decorative but lacks aria-hidden="true". Some screen readers may announce the arrow character in addition to the button text, creating redundant or confusing output.
issuesFound.fix: Line 754-757: ```tsx {isOpen ? '▲' : '▼'} ```

fpTitles: ContactForm email input aria-describedby references non-existent ID

issuesFound.title: Section component accepts labelledby prop but never applies it
issuesFound.explanation: The Section component accepts a labelledby prop intended for aria-labelledby but never applies it to the rendered element. This breaks the ability to create properly labelled landmark sections.
issuesFound.fix: Line 724: `<Tag id={id} aria-labelledby={labelledby} className={['page-section', className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')}>`

issuesFound.title: Modal has aria-modal on overlay instead of dialog element
issuesFound.explanation: The aria-modal="true" attribute is placed on the modal-overlay div, but it should be on the element with role="dialog". This confuses assistive technologies about which element is the modal dialog.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2071: `<div className="modal-overlay" onClick={onClose}>`
  Fix: Line 2074: `<div ref={dialogRef} role="dialog" aria-modal="true" aria-labelledby={titleId} aria-describedby={description ? descId : undefined} className={['modal-dialog', `modal-dialog--${size}`].join(' ')} onClick={(ev) => ev.stopPropagation()}>`

issuesFound.title: StatItem does not expose valueLabel for screen reader accessibility
issuesFound.explanation: The Stat interface includes valueLabel for screen reader friendly pronunciation of formatted values like "40,000+", but StatItem renders only the visual value without using valueLabel. Screen readers may announce "40,000+" inconsistently.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 979: `

{stat.value}

`

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel non-visible slides not hidden from assistive technology
issuesFound.explanation: Carousel slides that are not currently visible remain in the accessibility tree. Screen reader users may encounter all slides sequentially instead of only the active slide, creating a confusing experience.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1599: `className={['testimonial-slide', isVisible ? 'testimonial-slide--visible' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} aria-hidden={!isVisible}`
